title: Romantic Crisp Dutch Prawns Pilaf
description: A crispy and flavorful Dutch-inspired pilaf dish featuring raw prawns, served in a romantic setting.

ingredients.name: 1 small onion finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely so it doesn't overpower the dish.

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic minced
ingredients.notes: Use a garlic press or crush the garlic with a knife.

ingredients.name: 1 cup uncooked white rice
ingredients.notes: Use a short-grain rice to get the right consistency.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped very finely so it's evenly distributed.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for a milder flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground for the best flavor.

instructions: Pound the prawns in a mortar and pestle until they're slightly flattened.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute until fragrant.
Add the uncooked white rice and stir to coat with the oil and mix with the onion and garlic.
Add 2 cups of hot water to the rice mixture and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 15 minutes.
While the rice is cooking, toss the chopped parsley with the smoked paprika, kosher salt, and freshly ground black pepper in a small bowl.
After the rice has cooked for 10 minutes, fluff it with a fork and stir in the chopped parsley mixture.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the rice mixture to a serving dish and top with the pounded prawns.
Drizzle the chopped fresh dill over the prawns and serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
